questions.question: What is the main responsibility of a backend system in a web application?
options: A) Styling components in the browser
B) Handling business logic, data access, security, and server-side processing
C) Replacing DNS
D) Rendering CSS animations
questions.correctAnswer: B) Handling business logic, data access, security, and server-side processing

questions.question: Why is HTTP knowledge important for backend engineers?
options: A) Because APIs, clients, caching, and browser-server communication depend on it
B) It only matters for designers
C) Because databases require HTTP syntax
D) It replaces version control
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because APIs, clients, caching, and browser-server communication depend on it

questions.question: What does request validation protect against?
options: A) Only slow internet speeds
B) Invalid or malicious input reaching business logic and storage layers
C) Browser rendering issues
D) Git conflicts
questions.correctAnswer: B) Invalid or malicious input reaching business logic and storage layers

questions.question: Why do backend systems need databases?
options: A) To persist and query application data reliably over time
B) Only to render frontend templates
C) To replace authentication
D) To compile source code
questions.correctAnswer: A) To persist and query application data reliably over time

questions.question: What is the main benefit of indexing in a database?
options: A) It can improve lookup and filtering performance for targeted queries
B) It encrypts all tables automatically
C) It removes the need for schema design
D) It only helps frontend search bars
questions.correctAnswer: A) It can improve lookup and filtering performance for targeted queries

questions.question: What is the difference between authentication and authorization?
options: A) Authentication identifies the user, authorization determines allowed actions
B) Authorization happens before login and authentication after logout
C) They are identical
D) Only frontend apps need authorization
questions.correctAnswer: A) Authentication identifies the user, authorization determines allowed actions

questions.question: Why should passwords be hashed before storage?
options: A) To reduce exposure if stored credentials are leaked
B) To make login pages look better
C) Because databases cannot store strings
D) To replace multi-factor authentication
questions.correctAnswer: A) To reduce exposure if stored credentials are leaked

questions.question: What problem does caching usually solve in backend systems?
options: A) Reducing repeated expensive work or repeated data retrieval for frequently accessed results
B) Replacing persistent storage entirely
C) Styling dashboards
D) Eliminating all security concerns
questions.correctAnswer: A) Reducing repeated expensive work or repeated data retrieval for frequently accessed results

questions.question: Why is pagination commonly used in APIs?
options: A) To control response size and make large datasets more efficient to serve and consume
B) To replace sorting
C) To compile JSON faster
D) To avoid authentication
questions.correctAnswer: A) To control response size and make large datasets more efficient to serve and consume

questions.question: What is the role of middleware in many backend frameworks?
options: A) Running shared request-processing logic such as auth, logging, parsing, or validation
B) Replacing route handlers completely
C) Editing database rows directly without code
D) Serving only image files
questions.correctAnswer: A) Running shared request-processing logic such as auth, logging, parsing, or validation

questions.question: Why are background jobs or queues useful?
options: A) They move slower or retryable work out of the immediate request path
B) They replace APIs completely
C) They only matter for frontend rendering
D) They eliminate the need for databases
questions.correctAnswer: A) They move slower or retryable work out of the immediate request path

questions.question: What does idempotency help with in distributed systems?
options: A) Making repeated requests safer when retries happen
B) Improving CSS maintainability
C) Replacing transactions
D) Avoiding all failures
questions.correctAnswer: A) Making repeated requests safer when retries happen

questions.question: Why is structured logging valuable?
options: A) It makes logs more consistent and useful for search, debugging, and monitoring
B) It replaces exception handling
C) It stores user passwords securely
D) It only matters in development
questions.correctAnswer: A) It makes logs more consistent and useful for search, debugging, and monitoring

questions.question: What do metrics help teams understand?
options: A) System behavior such as throughput, latency, error rates, and resource usage
B) HTML semantics only
C) Git branch names
D) CSS selector performance in the browser
questions.correctAnswer: A) System behavior such as throughput, latency, error rates, and resource usage

questions.question: Why is rate limiting useful on APIs?
options: A) It can protect services from abuse and uncontrolled request volume
B) It automatically fixes database schema issues
C) It only improves frontend styling
D) It replaces authentication
questions.correctAnswer: A) It can protect services from abuse and uncontrolled request volume

questions.question: What is the value of automated tests for backend services?
options: A) They help verify logic and catch regressions as the codebase changes
B) They make deployment unnecessary
C) They replace code review completely
D) They only test UI animations
questions.correctAnswer: A) They help verify logic and catch regressions as the codebase changes

questions.question: Why is environment-based configuration important?
options: A) It lets the same application run with different settings across local, staging, and production environments
B) It removes the need for version control
C) It prevents every runtime error
D) It is only useful in desktop software
questions.correctAnswer: A) It lets the same application run with different settings across local, staging, and production environments

questions.question: What is the purpose of CI/CD for backend delivery?
options: A) To automate checks and releases so delivery becomes more repeatable and safer
B) To eliminate the need for monitoring
C) To replace application logs
D) To avoid using source control
questions.correctAnswer: A) To automate checks and releases so delivery becomes more repeatable and safer

questions.question: Why do backend engineers need to think about failure modes and recovery?
options: A) Because production systems will face bad inputs, dependency failures, and infrastructure issues
B) Because failure only happens in frontend code
C) Because retrying is always safe
D) Because databases cannot fail
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because production systems will face bad inputs, dependency failures, and infrastructure issues

questions.question: What is the strongest mindset for learning backend engineering?
options: A) Focus only on one framework and ignore system behavior
B) Build solid fundamentals, understand data and network boundaries, and ship real services end to end
C) Avoid debugging production-style issues
D) Skip security until after launch
questions.correctAnswer: B) Build solid fundamentals, understand data and network boundaries, and ship real services end to end

questions.question: Which HTTP method is idempotent?
options: A) POST
B) GET
C) PATCH
D) CONNECT
questions.correctAnswer: B) GET

questions.question: Which HTTP status code means Not Found?
